carbon_policy_signal.item: Ireland leads EU ETS reform negotiations H2 2026; outcome determines scheme tightness (free allocation cuts, MSR intake) and EUA floor/ceiling price support.
carbon_policy_signal.why_it_matters: Reform outcome directly sets EUA cost pass-through into power merit order and thermal generation competitiveness; weakening bearish-power, tightening bullish-power via carbon cost uplift.

freshness_caveat: Coal data 186 days stale (as of 2025-12-26); Clean Dark 181 days stale (as of 2025-12-31). Coal refresh critical to validate thermal stack positioning — current 7th-pctile may be outdated.
risk_flags: Clean Spark at 94th-pctile (43.04 EUR/MWh) signals extreme gas-to-power conversion premium; coal at 7th-pctile (96 USD/t, 186 days stale) — if coal data refreshes higher, thermal stack weakens.
GB Power at 92nd-pctile (128.38 EUR/MWh, +13.58% month); renewables at 28th-pctile (32.44% of load, -40% daily) — scarcity of wind/solar driving thermal dispatch.
Iran blocking Hormuz security mission elevates LNG shipping risk; TTF at 52nd-pctile (42.56 EUR/MWh) leaves limited room for upside shock absorption.
Ireland-led EU ETS reform outcome (weeks horizon) directly sets EUA floor/ceiling; bearish outcome could weaken carbon price support for thermal generation merit order.

base.summary: Renewable supply stays tight; thermal remains extended; Hormuz status quo. Power stable at elevated levels.

downside.summary: Renewable wind/solar forecast improves; heat wave breaks; Hormuz tensions ease. Thermal premium compresses sharply.

upside.summary: Iran escalates Hormuz blockade or maritime incident; LNG shipping cost spikes; TTF supply tightens. Heat-wave persistence. Power spiking.

themes: Thermal generation dominance — Clean Spark extended
GB-DE power premium elevated amid heat stress
Geopolitical LNG supply tail-risk via Hormuz
EU ETS reform negotiations — carbon scheme durability at stake
top_drivers: Renewables at 28th-pctile (-40% daily) — scarcity of wind/solar pushes thermal merit-order hierarchy; gas and coal both in-the-money despite coal at record lows.
GB Power at 92nd-pctile and Clean Spark at 94th-pctile — UK-DE supply tension, heat-wave peak load, and low renewable availability locking in thermal generation premium.
Hormuz geopolitical tail-risk elevates LNG supply insurance and shipping costs; Iran blocking maritime security mission constrains Middle East flow into EU and supports power/gas scarcity premium.
top_takeaway: Clean Spark at 94th-pctile; GB Power at 92nd-pctile; thermal generation massively in-the-money. Hormuz tension and heat-wave load surge underpin power rally.

watchlist.why: At 94th-pctile; coal data is 186 days stale — refresh will re-anchor thermal stack valuation and Cal+1 curve.
Down 40% daily to 28th-pctile; further weakness prolongs thermal-in-the-money regime and extends GB power premium.

watchlist.metric: Hormuz corridor LNG flows & insurance costs
watchlist.why: Iran rejects maritime security plan; escalation would spike shipping costs and compress LNG arbitrage into EU, supporting TTF/power indirectly.

watchlist.metric: France nuclear output during heat wave
watchlist.why: Record heat driving 1,000 deaths and cooling-water stress; outage risk bullish for intraday DA power and EUA thermal dispatch.
Ireland presidency H2 2026 — weakening scheme bearish-EUA and power merit order; tightening bullish-EUA and coal/gas cost pass-through.

watchlist_dated.name: France heat-wave peak load — DA auction
watchlist_dated.why: Extreme cooling demand bullish intraday power and EUA thermal dispatch uplift.
